title: quercus vacciniifolia
intro: Quercus vacciniifolia, also known as the Vaccinium oak or holly oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus vacciniifolia include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Quercus vacciniifolia include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions made from its leaves and bark, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
